content:
- Final required validation is green: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed after two explicit repo-boundary updates.
- Updated the task file to `<status>completed</status> <passes>true</passes>` and checked off the execution plan boxes.
- Ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, which updated `current_tasks.md` and `current_tasks_done.md` and selected the next task.
- Next: review the final diff/state, commit all relevant changes with the required task-finished message, push, and stop.

- Completed story-18 task 03 and pushed commit `13b56a9` to `master`.
- Implemented a dedicated scratch verify image at `cockroachdb_molt/molt/Dockerfile` plus verify-specific contract/harness coverage in `crates/runner/tests/verify_image_contract.rs`, `support/verify_docker_contract.rs`, and `support/verify_image_harness.rs`.
- Repo-wide follow-up fixes: allowed the new verify Dockerfile in `verify_source_contract.rs`, allowed the new verify image harness in `e2e_integrity_contract_support.rs`, and cleaned the verify command help text in `cockroachdb_molt/molt/cmd/verify/verify.go`.
- Required validation passed: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`.
- Updated the task file to completed with `<passes>true</passes>`, checked off the execution plan, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, and Ralph selected the next task.
- If you must quit immediately after this context window, the next turn should start with the normal startup workflow and read the newly selected Ralph task.

- Started Choose-Task turn.
- Read previous progress log and the list-active-tasks skill instructions.
- Next: refresh Ralph summary, inspect current active tasks and done tasks, resolve blockers, and write the selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and read current_tasks.md, current_tasks_done.md, and choose-task rules.
- Confirmed there are many active failing tasks and no active failing bugs shown in the summary so far.
- Next: inspect active task files for <priority> and <blocked_by> so selection is based on real gating, then write the chosen path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Evaluated active failing tasks after refreshing Ralph summaries; no unblocked failing bug tasks are active.
- Checked active task files for <priority> and <blocked_by>; only story-22a task 02 is explicitly blocked, and no active task has a higher priority tag.
- Determined the actual stack is a mixed Rust runner workspace plus a pruned Go verify slice in cockroachdb_molt/molt with Docker scratch-image packaging.
- Chose story-18 task 04 as the most logical next unblocked task because the verify scratch image was just completed and the next critical-path gap is the dedicated verify-service config needed before the HTTP API, metrics, and security tasks can proceed.
- Next turn should start from .ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/04-task-add-verify-service-config-for-source-destination-tls-and-mode-selection.md.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skills.
- Confirmed current task is story 18 task 04: dedicated verify-service config for source/destination TLS and verify mode selection.
- Determined the task is still pre-execution because the task file has no plan pointer yet; this turn should create the plan file, add the `<plan>` path to the task, mark the plan `TO BE VERIFIED`, then stop.
- Next: inspect the current verify source/config boundaries and existing plan/task conventions so the new plan is concrete and repo-consistent.

- Read story 18 task 04 and confirmed it had no plan yet, so this turn stayed in the required planning-only mode.
- Inspected the current Go verify boundary (`molt verify`, `cmdutil.DBConnFlags`, `dbconn.Config`, `dbconn.Connect`), the Rust runner config boundary that rejects legacy verify sections, task 05, and the improve-code-boundaries smell docs relevant to config reduction, validation ownership, wrong-placeism, and shared connection shapes.
- Created the task 04 plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/04-task-add-verify-service-config-for-source-destination-tls-and-mode-selection_plans/2026-04-19-verify-service-config-plan.md` and added the `<plan>` pointer to the task file.
- The plan keeps the task in `TO BE VERIFIED` state and proposes a dedicated `molt verify-service validate-config --config <path>` boundary, a Go-owned verify-service config package, explicit per-DB `verify-full|verify-ca` TLS modes, explicit listener `http|https` and `none|mtls` auth enums, config-only connection sourcing, and a final local Go plus required repo-lane verification sequence.
- I intentionally did not start implementation or run validation lanes because the task instructions require quitting immediately after producing the first plan.
- Next turn should review the plan file, decide whether the command/config shape is still correct, switch the plan footer to `NOW EXECUTE` only if approved, and then stop immediately again per the task workflow.

- Read prior Ralph progress, the current task pointer, the active task file, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed story 18 task 04 already has a plan pointer; this turn is determining whether the plan footer is still `TO BE VERIFIED` or has been promoted to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Next I am reading the referenced plan file and will follow the task workflow exactly: either stop immediately in plan-review state or execute the plan with strict red/green validation.

- Read the existing task plan and confirmed it still ends in `TO BE VERIFIED`, so this turn is still a plan-review gate rather than an implementation turn.
- Began verifying the proposed interface against the current Go verify command boundary, shared DB flag/config helpers, and the Rust runner config guardrail that rejects legacy verify config.
- Next I am checking the improve-code-boundaries reference files and current command/config code to decide whether the plan can safely be promoted to `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Confirmed the current Go verify command is still flag-driven through `cmdutil.RegisterDBConnFlags` / `LoadDBConns`, and `dbconn.Config` is only a raw `{Source, Target}` carrier.
- Confirmed the Rust runner config remains a separate boundary, which supports the plan decision to create a Go-owned verify-service config instead of reopening runner YAML.
- Next I am reading the improve-code-boundaries smell notes and existing Go test patterns to decide whether the current plan shape should be promoted to `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Read the main improve-code-boundaries smell references for wrong-place config growth, config-not-reduced, validation-outside-config, and shared connection shapes; they all support the existing task-04 plan.
- Cross-checked follow-up task 05 and existing Go test style so the plan remains scoped to a dedicated config boundary and does not prematurely absorb the HTTP API.
- Next I am doing a final feasibility check on Go YAML support and package layout; if that passes, I will promote the plan footer to `NOW EXECUTE` and stop immediately as required.

- Re-read story 18 task 04, its referenced plan, the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skills, the current Go verify/dbconn boundary, the Rust runner config guardrail, the follow-up task 05, and the relevant improve-code-boundaries smell notes.
- Confirmed the existing plan is sound: dedicated Go-owned verify-service config, no runner-config regression, config-only command surface, one shared validated DB shape, validation entirely inside config, and no premature HTTP API expansion.
- Promoted `.ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/04-task-add-verify-service-config-for-source-destination-tls-and-mode-selection_plans/2026-04-19-verify-service-config-plan.md` from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE` and stopped immediately per the task workflow.
- Next turn must read the now-executable plan, read the `tdd` skill again, execute the plan via strict red/green slices, run explicit local Go validation plus `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, and only then update the task file / switch task / commit / push.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, task 04 file, and the referenced plan now ending in NOW EXECUTE.
- Re-read the tdd and improve-code-boundaries skill instructions for this execution turn.
- Confirmed the current Go boundary is still old CLI flags via cmdutil/RegisterDBConnFlags and LoadDBConns; verify-service config and command do not exist yet.
- Next: inspect the existing Go command/test layout, add the first failing verify-service command/config test, then implement red-green slices from the plan.

- Executed the first TDD slices for story 18 task 04 in the Go verify slice.
- Added a new root-command constructor, a dedicated `molt verify-service validate-config --config <path>` command, and a new `verifyservice` config package with YAML loading.
- Added red/green coverage for: successful config validation, explicit DB TLS modes, passwordless client-cert auth plus cert/key pairing, listener https/mtls rules, warning output when direct service auth is disabled, and config-only help surface.
- Reduced a muddy boundary by deleting `cockroachdb_molt/molt/dbconn/config.go`; the old source/target string carrier now lives only inside `cmd/internal/cmdutil` where it is actually used.
- Focused Go tests for `./cmd`, `./verifyservice`, and `./cmd/internal/cmdutil` are green.
- Full `env GOTOOLCHAIN=auto go test ./...` from `cockroachdb_molt/molt` is not green in this environment because many pre-existing integration-style packages require local databases/services (`defaultdb`/MySQL) that are unavailable here; I did not mask those failures.
- Next: run required repo lanes `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, fix any code issues they surface, then do a final boundary pass and update the task artifacts if all required lanes pass.
